name: MOVING iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC.
business: Moving iMAGE TECHNOLOGIES INC. provides technology, products, and services to movie theater operators and sports and entertainment venues. The company offers project management, design, integration, installation, and procurement services for new builds and upgrades, along with proprietary products such as ADA-compliant accessibility products, Caddy cup holders and trays, CineQC quality control software, MiTranslator with augmented reality glasses, and a mobile cart for eSports and gaming.

exposures.sentence: Another example is a proprietary mobile cart we've developed to enable eSports and gaming in movie-theater auditoriums.

We have also developed a translator product and service that will enable moviegoers to watch a movie in any language that the film is available in, all in the same auditorium through a set of augmented reality glasses.
CineQC is MiT's quality assurance and remote access software designed to support cinema operations.
We offer a series of in-house designed and assembled lighting products and dimmers designed to reduce energy consumption in cinemas, theaters, and performance venues.

Direct View LED screens utilize a technology that is an alternative to the century-old streaming of projector light thrown onto screens; the LED screen is more akin to a giant television screen, and its use renders the projection booth and mezzanine unnecessary reducing construction costs.

catalysts.sentence: Backlog at June 30, 2025 was approximately $7.52 million, which represent orders currently planned for April 30, 2026 shipment.

tamClaims.claim: 2025 global box office revenues were about $30 billion in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Gower Street Analytics, 2025 Global box office revenues were about $30 billion in 2024.

tamClaims.claim: U.S./Canada market contributed approximately $8.8 billion, international market $21.2 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S./Canada market contributed approximately $8.8 billion. The international market accounted for the remaining $21.2 billion, or roughly 70% of the worldwide total.

tamClaims.claim: Number of U.S. movie screens declined from 39,000 in 2025 from 44,000 in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Association of Theatre Owners, the number of U.S. movie screens declined from 39,000 in 2025 from 44,000 in 2024 due the lingering impacts of COVID and the SAG/AFTRA strikes.

tamClaims.claim: 96% of world's cinema screens are digitized.
tamClaims.sentence: 96% of the world s cinema screens are digitized; the conversion to digital cinema began in 2006.

tamClaims.claim: Roughly the low-teens percentage of cinema screens worldwide were laser-equipped as of 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: Industry sources indicate that as of 2024, roughly the low-teens percentage of cinema screens worldwide were laser-equipped, with lamps representing less than one-fifth of new projector sales.
